summary: CA deleted successfully via admin GUI. TLS errors confirmed on MITM sites. But captive portal probes (HTTP) returned normal responses - no 302 redirect to RCD install page. Previous auto-restore was caused by non-admin PowerShell failing silently.

key_findings.ca_deletion: SUCCESS - admin GUI deletion persists, no auto-restore after 30s
key_findings.previous_failure_cause: PowerShell was NOT running as Administrator. Remove-Item appeared to succeed but registry deletion was denied.
key_findings.tls_error_google: CONFIRMED - google.com also goes through VT MITM (not bypass)
key_findings.captive_portal_redirect: NOT_TRIGGERED - HTTP probes returned normal 204/200, no 302 redirect

steps.name: Check CA installation
steps.actual: CA found in both LM and CU Root stores. Thumbprint: 47A677A1828522E2EF3C28876D7E7482BB164A9D

steps.name: Delete Root CA (admin GUI)
steps.actual: User deleted CA via certlm.msc and certmgr.msc with admin privileges

steps.name: Verify deletion immediately
steps.actual: CONFIRMED: No Plantynet CA found in any store

steps.name: Verify deletion after 30s
steps.actual: CONFIRMED: CA still deleted after 30s - no auto-restore

steps.name: HTTPS TLS error test
steps.actual: naver.com: TLS_ERROR - SSL/TLS trust relationship cannot be established. google.com: same TLS error (NOT bypass).

additional_tests.msftconnecttest: HTTP 200 - Content: Microsoft Connect Test, no redirect

conclusion: CA deletion with admin rights works and persists. VT MITM causes TLS errors for both naver.com and google.com when CA is absent. However, captive portal HTTP probes do not redirect to RCD install page - RCD mechanism may require additional trigger beyond just missing CA.
recommendation: Investigate RCD trigger conditions on Etap side. The UNKNOWN_CA condition is achieved but captive portal redirect is not happening. Possible causes: (1) RCD feature not enabled in current Etap config, (2) RCD only triggers on specific HTTP endpoints, (3) RCD requires etap-side detection of TLS handshake failure.
